title: New York Fed Statement on Close Cooperation with Iraq Audit Body

source.name: Federal Reserve Bank of New York

summary: The Federal Reserve Bank of New York issued a statement on July 1, 2011, confirming close cooperation with the Special Inspector General for Iraq Reconstruction (SIGIR) regarding audits of the Development Fund for Iraq (DFI). The statement notes that the NY Fed routinely obtains account holder approval before releasing information about foreign central bank accounts and has provided requested data promptly for prior audits. The cash shipments from the NY Fed to Iraq during the Coalition Provisional Authority tenure are described as a matter of public record.
key_points: NY Fed confirms close cooperation with SIGIR audits of Development Fund for Iraq accounts
Release of foreign central bank account information requires account holder approval
NY Fed states it has routinely obtained such approvals and provided data promptly in prior DFI audits
Cash shipments from NY Fed to Iraq during CPA tenure are described as long-standing public record
SIGIR Special Inspector General Stuart W. Bowen Jr. describes relationship as positive and effective over seven years
Contact listed for media inquiries: Jack Gutt (212) 720-6142
why_it_matters: This statement is not relevant to market structure or macro catalysts. It is an administrative disclosure regarding cooperation with the Special Inspector General for Iraq Reconstruction (SIGIR) for audits of the Development Fund for Iraq. Contains no information about monetary policy, interest rates, market operations, or economic indicators.
